'''車大歳神社の翁舞'''（くるまおおとしじんじゃのおきなまい）は、[[兵庫県]][[神戸市]][[須磨区]]車に伝わる民俗芸能の翁舞。
[[2000年]][[12月27日]]に[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定された。
== 関連項目 == *[[伝統芸能]] *[[奈良豆比古神社の翁舞]]

revision.comment: とりあえず神社の説明に
revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=車大歳神社| 所在地=兵庫県神戸市須磨区車字松ヶ原551| 祭神=大歳御祖神| 創建=大化2年（646年）| 例祭=[[10月16日]]| 神事=翁舞神事（[[1月14日]]）}}
'''車大歳神社'''（くるまおおとしじんじゃ）は、[[兵庫県]][[神戸市]][[須磨区]]車にある[[神社]]である。
[[年神|大歳御祖神]]（おおとしみおやのかみ）を祀る。
現在は神職が常駐しておらず、[[兵庫区]]上祇園町の祇園神社宮司が兼務している。
==歴史== 由緒は不明であるが、『武庫郡誌』には[[大化]]2年（[[646年]]）の創建と記載されている。[[応永]]4年（[[1397年]]）、[[摂津国]][[八部郡]]丹生郷より遷座したと伝えられる。
==祭事== 毎年[[1月14日]]に行われる翁舞は、[[2000年]][[12月27日]]に[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定された。
== 関連項目 == *[[伝統芸能]] *[[奈良豆比古神社の翁舞]]
==外部リンク== *[http://www.kobe-gionjinjya.com/index_o.html 車大歳神社]（祇園神社による）
[[Category:兵庫県の神社|くるまおおとししんしや]] [[Category:神戸市|くるまおおとししんしや]] [[Category:伝統芸能|くるまおおとししんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

==歴史== 由緒は不明であるが、『武庫郡誌』には[[大化]]2年（[[646年]]）の創建と記載されている。[[応永]]4年（[[1397年]]）、[[摂津国]][[八部郡]]丹生郷より遷座したとも伝えられる。

[[Category:兵庫県の神社|くるまおおとししんしや]] [[Category:神戸市|くるまおおとししんしや]] [[Category:伝統芸能|くるまおおとししんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=車大歳神社| 所在地=兵庫県神戸市須磨区車字松ヶ原551| 祭神=大歳御祖神| 創建=大化2年（646年）| 例祭=[[10月16日]]| 神事=翁舞神事（[[1月14日]]）}}
'''車大歳神社'''（くるまおおとしじんじゃ）は、[[兵庫県]][[神戸市]][[須磨区]]車にある[[神社]]である。
[[年神|大歳御祖神]]（おおとしみおやのかみ）を祀る。
現在は神職が常駐しておらず、[[兵庫区]]上祇園町の祇園神社宮司が兼務している。
==歴史== 由緒は不明であるが、『武庫郡誌』には[[大化]]2年（[[646年]]）の創建と記載されている。[[応永]]4年（[[1397年]]）、[[摂津国]][[八部郡]]丹生郷より遷座したとも伝えられる。
==祭事== [[画像:車翁舞.JPG|thumb|170px|車大歳神社の翁舞]] 毎年[[1月14日]]に翁舞が行われる。一般的な翁舞い（[[式三番]]）が千鳥・翁・三番叟という構成であるのに対し、ここでの翁舞は露払・翁・三番叟・父尉という構成であり、父尉を省略しない形式が残っているとして、[[2000年]][[12月27日]]に[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定された。
舞に用いられる面は、[[神体]]として本殿に祀られている物で、翁・黒式尉・父尉の三種がある。本番当日、宿（頭家）に神体が迎えられ、宿で稽古上げが行われる。その後宮入りを行い、舞台（拝殿）において翁舞が行われる。
== 関連項目 == *[[伝統芸能]] *[[奈良豆比古神社の翁舞]]
==外部リンク== *[http://www.kobe-gionjinjya.com/index_o.html 車大歳神社]（祇園神社による） *[http://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~an3t-ingk/okina.pdf 銀輪散歩 車の翁舞]
[[Category:兵庫県の神社|くるまおおとししんしや]] [[Category:神戸市|くるまおおとししんしや]] [[Category:伝統芸能|くるまおおとししんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社 |名称 = 車大歳神社 |画像 = [[File:Kuruma-ohtoshi-jinja.jpg|300px]] |所在地 = 兵庫県神戸市須磨区車字松ヶ原551 {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|41|0.9|N|135|6|49.8|E|region:JP}} |祭神 = 大歳御祖神 |社格 = |創建 = 大化2年（646年） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[10月16日]] |神事 = 翁舞神事（[[1月14日]]） }}
'''車大歳神社'''（くるまおおとしじんじゃ）は、[[兵庫県]][[神戸市]][[須磨区]]車にある[[神社]]である。 現在は神職が常駐しておらず、[[兵庫区]]上祇園町の祇園神社宮司が兼務している。
== 祭神 == * [[年神|大歳御祖神]]（おおとしみおやのかみ）を祀る。 ==歴史== 由緒は不明であるが、『武庫郡誌』には[[大化]]2年（[[646年]]）の創建と記載されている。[[応永]]4年（[[1397年]]）、[[摂津国]][[八部郡]]丹生郷より遷座したとも伝えられる。
==祭事== 毎年[[1月14日]]に行われる[[車大歳神社の翁舞|翁舞神事]]は、[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定されている。
== ご朱印 == *ご朱印は、兼務社である祇園神社でやっておらず、祇園神社が指定した車大歳神社総代宅へ電話連絡することになる。
== 交通アクセス == *[[神戸市営地下鉄西神・山手線]][[妙法寺駅 (兵庫県)|妙法寺駅]]より、北に徒歩17分。
== 関連項目 == *[[奈良豆比古神社]]（[[奈良豆比古神社の翁舞|翁舞]]）
==外部リンク== *[http://www.kobe-gionjinjya.com/ohtoshi.html 車大歳神社]（祇園神社による）
{{DEFAULTSORT:くるまおおとししんしや}} [[Category:神戸市の神社]] [[Category:須磨区の歴史]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{Shrine |Name = Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine |Image = [[File:Kuruma-ohtoshi-jinja.jpg|300px]] |Location = [[Hyogo Prefecture]], [[Kobe City]], [[Suma Ward]], Kuruma Matsugahara 551 {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|41|0.9|N|135|6|49.8|E|region:JP-28-type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = [[Toshigami|Ōtoshi Mioya no Kami]] |Shrine rank = |Founded = [[Taika]] 2 ([[646]]) |Main hall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = [[October 16]] |Rituals = Okina Dance Ritual ([[January 14]]) |Map = {{Location map|Kobe#Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=41|lat_sec=0.9|lon_deg=135|lon_min=6|lon_sec=49.8|label=Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} [[ja:車大歳神社]] '''Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine''' (車大歳神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the Kuruma district of [[Suma Ward]], [[Kobe City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]. Currently, it does not have a resident priest; the chief priest of [[Gion Shrine]] in Kamigion-chō, [[Hyogo Ward]], oversees its administration.
== Enshrined Deity == * [[Toshigami|Ōtoshi Mioya no Kami]] (大歳御祖神)
== History == While the origins of the shrine are unclear, ''Mukogun-shi'' records its founding in the second year of [[Taika]] (646). It is also said to have been relocated from Nyūgo, [[Yabe District]], [[Settsu Province]], in [[Ōei]] 4 (1397).
== Festivals == The Okina Dance Ritual (翁舞神事), held annually on [[January 14]], is designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].
== Goshuin (Stamp) == * Goshuin can only be obtained by contacting the Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine representative's residence, as designated by the managing Gion Shrine. Gion Shrine itself does not issue the goshuin.
== Access == * A 17-minute walk north from [[Myōhōji Station]] on the [[Kobe Municipal Subway Seishin-Yamate Line]].
== Related Articles == * [[Narabehi Shrine]] (Okina Dance)
== External Links == * [http://www.kobe-gionjinjya.com/ohtoshi.html Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine] (via Gion Shrine)
{{DEFAULTSORT:Kuruma Otoshi Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:History of Suma Ward]] {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Buildings in Suma Ward]]

{{Shrine |Name = Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine |Image = [[File:Kuruma-ohtoshi-jinja.jpg|300px]] |Location = [[Hyogo Prefecture]], [[Kobe City]], [[Suma Ward]], Kuruma Matsugahara 551 {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|41|0.9|N|135|6|49.8|E|region:JP-28-type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = [[Toshigami|Ōtoshi Mioya no Kami]] |Shrine rank = |Founded = [[Taika]] 2 ([[646]]) |Main hall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = [[October 16]] |Rituals = Okina Dance Ritual ([[January 14]]) |Map = {{Location map|Kobe#Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=41|lat_sec=0.9|lon_deg=135|lon_min=6|lon_sec=49.8|label=Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} [[ja:車大歳神社]] '''Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine''' (車大歳神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the Kuruma district of [[Suma Ward]], [[Kobe City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]. Currently, it does not have a resident priest; the chief priest of [[Gion Shrine]] in Kamigion-chō, [[Hyogo Ward]], oversees its administration.
== Enshrined Deity == * [[Toshigami|Ōtoshi Mioya no Kami]] (大歳御祖神)
== History == While the origins of the shrine are unclear, ''Mukogun-shi'' records its founding in the second year of [[Taika]] (646). It is also said to have been relocated from Nyūgo, [[Yabe District]], [[Settsu Province]], in [[Ōei]] 4 (1397).
== Festivals == The Okina Dance Ritual (翁舞神事), held annually on [[January 14]], is designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].
== Goshuin (Stamp) == * Goshuin can only be obtained by contacting the Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine representative's residence, as designated by the managing Gion Shrine. Gion Shrine itself does not issue the goshuin.
== Access == * A 17-minute walk north from [[Myōhōji Station]] on the [[Kobe Municipal Subway Seishin-Yamate Line]].
== Related Articles == * [[Narabehi Shrine]] (Okina Dance)
== External Links == * [http://www.kobe-gionjinjya.com/ohtoshi.html Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine] (via Gion Shrine)

revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kuruma-ohtoshi-jinja.jpg|300px]] |location = [[Hyogo Prefecture]], [[Kobe City]], [[Suma Ward]], Kuruma Matsugahara 551 {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|41|0.9|N|135|6|49.8|E|region:JP-28-type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = [[Toshigami|Ōtoshi Mioya no Kami]] |Shrine rank = |consecration_year = [[Taika]] 2 ([[646]]) |Main hall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = [[October 16]] |Rituals = Okina Dance Ritual ([[January 14]]) |Map = {{location map|Kobe#Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=41|lat_sec=0.9|lon_deg=135|lon_min=6|lon_sec=49.8|label=Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} [[ja:車大歳神社]] '''Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine''' (車大歳神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the Kuruma district of [[Suma Ward]], [[Kobe City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]. Currently, it does not have a resident priest; the chief priest of [[Gion Shrine]] in Kamigion-chō, [[Hyogo Ward]], oversees its administration.
== deity == * [[Toshigami|Ōtoshi Mioya no Kami]] (大歳御祖神)
== History == While the origins of the shrine are unclear, ''Mukogun-shi'' records its founding in the second year of [[Taika]] (646). It is also said to have been relocated from Nyūgo, [[Yabe District]], [[Settsu Province]], in [[Ōei]] 4 (1397).
== Festivals == The Okina Dance Ritual (翁舞神事), held annually on [[January 14]], is designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].
== Goshuin (Stamp) == * Goshuin can only be obtained by contacting the Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine representative's residence, as designated by the managing Gion Shrine. Gion Shrine itself does not issue the goshuin.
== Access == * A 17-minute walk north from [[Myōhōji Station]] on the [[Kobe Municipal Subway Seishin-Yamate Line]].
== Related Articles == * [[Narabehi Shrine]] (Okina Dance)
== External Links == * [http://www.kobe-gionjinjya.com/ohtoshi.html Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine] (via Gion Shrine)

{{Shrine |name = Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kuruma-ohtoshi-jinja.jpg|300px]] |location = [[Hyogo Prefecture]], [[Kobe City]], [[Suma Ward]], Kuruma Matsugahara 551 {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|41|0.9|N|135|6|49.8|E|region:JP-28-type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = [[Toshigami|Ōtoshi Mioya no Kami]] |Shrine rank = |consecration_year = [[Taika]] 2 ([[646]]) |Main hall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = [[October 16]] |Rituals = Okina Dance Ritual ([[January 14]]) |Map = {{location map|Kobe#Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=41|lat_sec=0.9|lon_deg=135|lon_min=6|lon_sec=49.8|label=Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} [[ja:車大歳神社]] '''Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine''' (車大歳神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the Kuruma district of [[Suma Ward]], [[Kobe City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]. Currently, it does not have a resident priest; the chief priest of [[Gion Shrine]] in Kamigion-chō, [[Hyogo Ward]], oversees its administration.
== deity == * [[Toshigami|Ōtoshi Mioya no Kami]] (大歳御祖神)
== History == While the origins of the shrine are unclear, ''Mukogun-shi'' records its founding in the second year of [[Taika]] (646). It is also said to have been relocated from Nyūgo, [[Yabe District]], [[Settsu Province]], in [[Ōei]] 4 (1397).
== Festivals == The Okina Dance Ritual (翁舞神事), held annually on [[January 14]], is designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].
== Goshuin (Stamp) == * Goshuin can only be obtained by contacting the Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine representative's residence, as designated by the managing Gion Shrine. Gion Shrine itself does not issue the goshuin.
== Access == * A 17-minute walk north from [[Myōhōji Station]] on the [[Kobe Municipal Subway Seishin-Yamate Line]].
== Related Articles == * [[Narabehi Shrine]] (Okina Dance)
== External Links == * [http://www.kobe-gionjinjya.com/ohtoshi.html Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine] (via Gion Shrine)

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kuruma-ohtoshi-jinja.jpg|300px]] |location = [[Hyogo Prefecture]], [[Kobe City]], [[Suma Ward]], Kuruma Matsugahara 551 {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|41|0.9|N|135|6|49.8|E|region:JP-28-type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = [[Toshigami|Ōtoshi Mioya no Kami]] |Shrine rank = |consecration_year = [[Taika]] 2 ([[646]]) |Main hall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = [[October 16]] |Rituals = Okina Dance Ritual ([[January 14]]) |Map = {{location map|Kobe#Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=41|lat_sec=0.9|lon_deg=135|lon_min=6|lon_sec=49.8|label=Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} [[ja:車大歳神社]] '''Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine''' (車大歳神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the Kuruma district of [[Suma Ward]], [[Kobe City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]. Currently, it does not have a resident priest; the chief priest of [[Gion Shrine]] in Kamigion-chō, [[Hyogo Ward]], oversees its administration.
== deity == * [[Toshigami|Ōtoshi Mioya no Kami]] (大歳御祖神)
== History == While the origins of the shrine are unclear, ''Mukogun-shi'' records its founding in the second year of [[Taika]] (646). It is also said to have been relocated from Nyūgo, [[Yabe District]], [[Settsu Province]], in [[Ōei]] 4 (1397).
== Festivals == The Okina Dance Ritual (翁舞神事), held annually on [[January 14]], is designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].
== Goshuin (Stamp) == * Goshuin can only be obtained by contacting the Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine representative's residence, as designated by the managing Gion Shrine. Gion Shrine itself does not issue the goshuin.
== Access == * A 17-minute walk north from [[Myōhōji Station]] on the [[Kobe Municipal Subway Seishin-Yamate Line]].
== Related Articles == * [[Narabehi Shrine]] (Okina Dance)
== External Links == * [http://www.kobe-gionjinjya.com/ohtoshi.html Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine] (via Gion Shrine)

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kuruma-ohtoshi-jinja.jpg|300px]] |location = [[Hyogo Prefecture]], [[Kobe City]], [[Suma Ward]], Kuruma Matsugahara 551 {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|41|0.9|N|135|6|49.8|E|region:JP-28-type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = [[Toshigami|Ōtoshi Mioya no Kami]] |Shrine rank = |consecration_year = [[Taika]] 2 ([[646]]) |Main hall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = [[October 16]] |Rituals = Okina Dance Ritual ([[January 14]]) |Map = {{location map|Kobe#Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=41|lat_sec=0.9|lon_deg=135|lon_min=6|lon_sec=49.8|label=Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} [[ja:車大歳神社]] '''Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine''' (車大歳神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the Kuruma district of [[Suma Ward]], [[Kobe City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]. Currently, it does not have a resident priest; the chief priest of [[Gion Shrine]] in Kamigion-chō, [[Hyogo Ward]], oversees its administration.
== deity == * [[Toshigami|Ōtoshi Mioya no Kami]] (大歳御祖神)
== History == While the origins of the shrine are unclear, ''Mukogun-shi'' records its founding in the second year of [[Taika]] (646). It is also said to have been relocated from Nyūgo, [[Yabe District]], [[Settsu Province]], in [[Ōei]] 4 (1397).
== Festivals == The Okina Dance Ritual (翁舞神事), held annually on [[January 14]], is designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].
== Goshuin (Stamp) == * Goshuin can only be obtained by contacting the Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine representative's residence, as designated by the managing Gion Shrine. Gion Shrine itself does not issue the goshuin.
== Access == * A 17-minute walk north from [[Myōhōji Station]] on the [[Kobe Municipal Subway Seishin-Yamate Line]].
== Related Articles == * [[Narabehi Shrine]] (Okina Dance)
== External Links == * [http://www.kobe-gionjinjya.com/ohtoshi.html Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine] (via Gion Shrine)

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kuruma-ohtoshi-jinja.jpg|300px]] |location = [[Hyogo Prefecture]], [[Kobe City]], [[Suma Ward]], Kuruma Matsugahara 551 {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|41|0.9|N|135|6|49.8|E|region:JP-28-type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = [[Toshigami|Ōtoshi Mioya no Kami]] |Shrine rank = |consecration_year = [[Taika]] 2 ([[646]]) |Main hall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = [[October 16]] |Rituals = Okina Dance Ritual ([[January 14]]) |Map = {{location map|Kobe#Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=41|lat_sec=0.9|lon_deg=135|lon_min=6|lon_sec=49.8|label=Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} [[ja:車大歳神社]] '''Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine''' (車大歳神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the Kuruma district of [[Suma Ward]], [[Kobe City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]. Currently, it does not have a resident priest; the chief priest of [[Gion Shrine]] in Kamigion-chō, [[Hyogo Ward]], oversees its administration.
== deity == * [[Toshigami|Ōtoshi Mioya no Kami]] (大歳御祖神)
== History == While the origins of the shrine are unclear, ''Mukogun-shi'' records its founding in the second year of [[Taika]] (646). It is also said to have been relocated from Nyūgo, [[Yabe District]], [[Settsu Province]], in [[Ōei]] 4 (1397).
== Festivals == The Okina Dance Ritual (翁舞神事), held annually on [[January 14]], is designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].
== Goshuin (Stamp) == * Goshuin can only be obtained by contacting the Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine representative's residence, as designated by the managing Gion Shrine. Gion Shrine itself does not issue the goshuin.
== Access == * A 17-minute walk north from [[Myōhōji Station]] on the [[Kobe Municipal Subway Seishin-Yamate Line]].
== Related Articles == * [[Narabehi Shrine]] (Okina Dance)
== External Links == * [http://www.kobe-gionjinjya.com/ohtoshi.html Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine] (via Gion Shrine)

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kuruma-ohtoshi-jinja.jpg|300px]] |location = [[Hyogo Prefecture]], [[Kobe City]], [[Suma Ward]], Kuruma Matsugahara 551 {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|41|0.9|N|135|6|49.8|E|region:JP-28-type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = [[Toshigami|Ōtoshi Mioya no Kami]] |Shrine rank = |consecration_year = [[Taika]] 2 ([[646]]) |Main hall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = [[October 16]] |Rituals = Okina Dance Ritual ([[January 14]]) |Map = {{location map|Kobe#Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=41|lat_sec=0.9|lon_deg=135|lon_min=6|lon_sec=49.8|label=Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} [[ja:車大歳神社]] '''Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine''' (車大歳神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the Kuruma district of [[Suma Ward]], [[Kobe City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]. Currently, it does not have a resident priest; the chief priest of [[Gion Shrine]] in Kamigion-chō, [[Hyogo Ward]], oversees its administration.
== deity == * [[Toshigami|Ōtoshi Mioya no Kami]] (大歳御祖神)
== History == While the origins of the shrine are unclear, ''Mukogun-shi'' records its founding in the second year of [[Taika]] (646). It is also said to have been relocated from Nyūgo, [[Yabe District]], [[Settsu Province]], in [[Ōei]] 4 (1397).
== Festivals == The Okina Dance Ritual (翁舞神事), held annually on [[January 14]], is designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].
== Goshuin (Stamp) == * Goshuin can only be obtained by contacting the Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine representative's residence, as designated by the managing Gion Shrine. Gion Shrine itself does not issue the goshuin.
== Access == * A 17-minute walk north from [[Myōhōji Station]] on the [[Kobe Municipal Subway Seishin-Yamate Line]].
== Related Articles == * [[Narabehi Shrine]] (Okina Dance)
== External Links == * [http://www.kobe-gionjinjya.com/ohtoshi.html Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine] (via Gion Shrine)

Currently, it does not have a resident priest; the chief priest of [[Gion Shrine]] in Kamigion-chō, [[Hyogo Ward]], oversees its administration.
== deity == * [[Toshigami|Ōtoshi Mioya no Kami]] (大歳御祖神)
== History == While the origins of the shrine are unclear, ''Mukogun-shi'' records its founding in the second year of [[Taika]] (646). It is also said to have been relocated from Nyūgo, [[Yabe District]], [[Settsu Province]], in [[Ōei]] 4 (1397).
== Festivals == The Okina Dance Ritual (翁舞神事), held annually on [[January 14]], is designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].
== Goshuin (Stamp) == * Goshuin can only be obtained by contacting the Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine representative's residence, as designated by the managing Gion Shrine. Gion Shrine itself does not issue the goshuin.
== Access == * A 17-minute walk north from [[Myōhōji Station]] on the [[Kobe Municipal Subway Seishin-Yamate Line]].
== Related Articles == * [[Narabehi Shrine]] (Okina Dance)

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kuruma-ohtoshi-jinja.jpg|300px]] |location = [[Hyogo Prefecture]], [[Kobe City]], [[Suma Ward]], Kuruma Matsugahara 551 {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|41|0.9|N|135|6|49.8|E|region:JP-28-type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = [[Toshigami|Ōtoshi Mioya no Kami]] |Shrine rank = |consecration_year = [[Taika]] 2 ([[646]]) |Main hall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = [[October 16]] |Rituals = Okina Dance Ritual ([[January 14]]) |Map = {{location map|Kobe#Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=41|lat_sec=0.9|lon_deg=135|lon_min=6|lon_sec=49.8|label=Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} [[ja:車大歳神社]] '''Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine''' (車大歳神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the Kuruma district of [[Suma Ward]], [[Kobe City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]. Currently, it does not have a resident priest; the chief priest of [[Gion Shrine]] in Kamigion-chō, [[Hyogo Ward]], oversees its administration.
== deity == * [[Toshigami|Ōtoshi Mioya no Kami]] (大歳御祖神)
== History == While the origins of the shrine are unclear, ''Mukogun-shi'' records its founding in the second year of [[Taika]] (646). It is also said to have been relocated from Nyūgo, [[Yabe District]], [[Settsu Province]], in [[Ōei]] 4 (1397).
== Festivals == The Okina Dance Ritual (翁舞神事), held annually on [[January 14]], is designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].
== Goshuin (Stamp) == * Goshuin can only be obtained by contacting the Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine representative's residence, as designated by the managing Gion Shrine. Gion Shrine itself does not issue the goshuin.
== Access == * A 17-minute walk north from [[Myōhōji Station]] on the [[Kobe Municipal Subway Seishin-Yamate Line]].
== Related Articles == * [[Narabehi Shrine]] (Okina Dance)
== External Links == * [http://www.kobe-gionjinjya.com/ohtoshi.html Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine] (via Gion Shrine)

== deity == * [[Toshigami|Ōtoshi Mioya no Kami]] (大歳御祖神)
== History == While the origins of the shrine are unclear, ''Mukogun-shi'' records its founding in the second year of [[Taika]] (646). It is also said to have been relocated from Nyūgo, [[Yabe District]], [[Settsu Province]], in [[Ōei]] 4 (1397).

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kuruma-ohtoshi-jinja.jpg|300px]] |location = [[Hyogo Prefecture]], [[Kobe City]], [[Suma Ward]], Kuruma Matsugahara 551 {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|41|0.9|N|135|6|49.8|E|region:JP-28-type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = [[Toshigami|Ōtoshi Mioya no Kami]] |Shrine rank = |consecration_year = [[Taika]] 2 ([[646]]) |Main hall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = [[October 16]] |Rituals = Okina Dance Ritual ([[January 14]]) |Map = {{location map|Kobe#Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=41|lat_sec=0.9|lon_deg=135|lon_min=6|lon_sec=49.8|label=Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} [[ja:車大歳神社]] '''Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine''' (車大歳神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the Kuruma district of [[Suma Ward]], [[Kobe City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]. Currently, it does not have a resident priest; the chief priest of [[Gion Shrine]] in Kamigion-chō, [[Hyogo Ward]], oversees its administration.
== deity == * [[Toshigami|Ōtoshi Mioya no Kami]] (大歳御祖神)
== History == While the origins of the shrine are unclear, ''Mukogun-shi'' records its founding in the second year of [[Taika]] (646). It is also said to have been relocated from Nyūgo, [[Yabe District]], [[Settsu Province]], in [[Ōei]] 4 (1397).
== Festivals == The Okina Dance Ritual (翁舞神事), held annually on [[January 14]], is designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].
== Goshuin (Stamp) == * Goshuin can only be obtained by contacting the Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine representative's residence, as designated by the managing Gion Shrine. Gion Shrine itself does not issue the goshuin.
== Access == * A 17-minute walk north from [[Myōhōji Station]] on the [[Kobe Municipal Subway Seishin-Yamate Line]].
== Related Articles == * [[Narabehi Shrine]] (Okina Dance)
== External Links == * [http://www.kobe-gionjinjya.com/ohtoshi.html Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine] (via Gion Shrine)

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kuruma-ohtoshi-jinja.jpg|300px]] |location = [[Hyogo Prefecture]], [[Kobe City]], [[Suma Ward]], Kuruma Matsugahara 551 {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|41|0.9|N|135|6|49.8|E|region:JP-28-type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = [[Toshigami|Ōtoshi Mioya no Kami]] |Shrine rank = |consecration_year = [[Taika]] 2 ([[646]]) |Main hall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = [[October 16]] |Rituals = Okina Dance Ritual ([[January 14]]) |Map = {{location map|Kobe#Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=41|lat_sec=0.9|lon_deg=135|lon_min=6|lon_sec=49.8|label=Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} [[ja:車大歳神社]] '''Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine''' (車大歳神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the Kuruma district of [[Suma Ward]], [[Kobe City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]. Currently, it does not have a resident priest; the chief priest of [[Gion Shrine]] in Kamigion-chō, [[Hyogo Ward]], oversees its administration.
== deity == * [[Toshigami|Ōtoshi Mioya no Kami]] (大歳御祖神)
== History == While the origins of the shrine are unclear, ''Mukogun-shi'' records its founding in the second year of [[Taika]] (646). It is also said to have been relocated from Nyūgo, [[Yabe District]], [[Settsu Province]], in [[Ōei]] 4 (1397).
== Festivals == The Okina Dance Ritual (翁舞神事), held annually on [[January 14]], is designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].
== Goshuin (Stamp) == * Goshuin can only be obtained by contacting the Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine representative's residence, as designated by the managing Gion Shrine. Gion Shrine itself does not issue the goshuin.
== Access == * A 17-minute walk north from [[Myōhōji Station]] on the [[Kobe Municipal Subway Seishin-Yamate Line]].
== Related Articles == * [[Narabehi Shrine]] (Okina Dance)
== External Links == * [http://www.kobe-gionjinjya.com/ohtoshi.html Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine] (via Gion Shrine)

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kuruma-ohtoshi-jinja.jpg|300px]] |location = [[Hyogo Prefecture]], [[Kobe City]], [[Suma Ward]], Kuruma Matsugahara 551 {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|41|0.9|N|135|6|49.8|E|region:JP-28-type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = [[Toshigami|Ōtoshi Mioya no Kami]] |Shrine rank = |consecration_year = [[Taika]] 2 ([[646]]) |Main hall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = [[October 16]] |Rituals = Okina Dance Ritual ([[January 14]]) |Map = {{location map|Kobe#Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=41|lat_sec=0.9|lon_deg=135|lon_min=6|lon_sec=49.8|label=Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} [[ja:車大歳神社]] '''Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine''' (車大歳神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the Kuruma district of [[Suma Ward]], [[Kobe City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]. Currently, it does not have a resident priest; the chief priest of [[Gion Shrine]] in Kamigion-chō, [[Hyogo Ward]], oversees its administration.
== deity == * [[Toshigami|Ōtoshi Mioya no Kami]] (大歳御祖神)
== History == While the origins of the shrine are unclear, ''Mukogun-shi'' records its founding in the second year of [[Taika]] (646). It is also said to have been relocated from Nyūgo, [[Yabe District]], [[Settsu Province]], in [[Ōei]] 4 (1397).
== Festivals == The Okina Dance Ritual (翁舞神事), held annually on [[January 14]], is designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].
== Goshuin (Stamp) == * Goshuin can only be obtained by contacting the Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine representative's residence, as designated by the managing Gion Shrine. Gion Shrine itself does not issue the goshuin.
== Access == * A 17-minute walk north from [[Myōhōji Station]] on the [[Kobe Municipal Subway Seishin-Yamate Line]].
== Related Articles == * [[Narabehi Shrine]] (Okina Dance)
== External Links == * [http://www.kobe-gionjinjya.com/ohtoshi.html Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine] (via Gion Shrine)

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kuruma-ohtoshi-jinja.jpg|300px]] |location = [[Hyogo Prefecture]], [[Kobe City]], [[Suma Ward]], Kuruma Matsugahara 551 {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|41|0.9|N|135|6|49.8|E|region:JP-28-type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = [[Toshigami|Ōtoshi Mioya no Kami]] |Shrine rank = |consecration_year = [[Taika]] 2 ([[646]]) |Main hall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = [[October 16]] |Rituals = Okina Dance Ritual ([[January 14]]) |Map = {{location map|Kobe#Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=41|lat_sec=0.9|lon_deg=135|lon_min=6|lon_sec=49.8|label=Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} [[ja:車大歳神社]] '''Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine''' (車大歳神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the Kuruma district of [[Suma Ward]], [[Kobe City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]. Currently, it does not have a resident priest; the chief priest of [[Gion Shrine]] in Kamigion-chō, [[Hyogo Ward]], oversees its administration.
== deity == * [[Toshigami|Ōtoshi Mioya no Kami]] (大歳御祖神)
== History == While the origins of the shrine are unclear, ''Mukogun-shi'' records its founding in the second year of [[Taika]] (646). It is also said to have been relocated from Nyūgo, [[Yabe District]], [[Settsu Province]], in [[Ōei]] 4 (1397).
== Festivals == The Okina Dance Ritual (翁舞神事), held annually on [[January 14]], is designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].
== Goshuin (Stamp) == * Goshuin can only be obtained by contacting the Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine representative's residence, as designated by the managing Gion Shrine. Gion Shrine itself does not issue the goshuin.
== Access == * A 17-minute walk north from [[Myōhōji Station]] on the [[Kobe Municipal Subway Seishin-Yamate Line]].
== Related Articles == * [[Narabehi Shrine]] (Okina Dance)
== External Links == * [http://www.kobe-gionjinjya.com/ohtoshi.html Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine] (via Gion Shrine)

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kuruma-ohtoshi-jinja.jpg|300px]] |location = [[Hyogo Prefecture]], [[Kobe City]], [[Suma Ward]], Kuruma Matsugahara 551 {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|41|0.9|N|135|6|49.8|E|region:JP-28-type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = [[Toshigami|Ōtoshi Mioya no Kami]] |Shrine rank = |consecration_year = [[Taika]] 2 ([[646]]) |Main hall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = [[October 16]] |Rituals = Okina Dance Ritual ([[January 14]]) |Map = {{location map|Kobe#Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=41|lat_sec=0.9|lon_deg=135|lon_min=6|lon_sec=49.8|label=Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }}
'''Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine''' (車大歳神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the Kuruma district of [[Suma Ward]], [[Kobe City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]. Currently, it does not have a resident priest; the chief priest of [[Gion Shrine]] in Kamigion-chō, [[Hyogo Ward]], oversees its administration.
== deity == * [[Toshigami|Ōtoshi Mioya no Kami]] (大歳御祖神)
== History == While the origins of the shrine are unclear, ''Mukogun-shi'' records its founding in the second year of [[Taika]] (646). It is also said to have been relocated from Nyūgo, [[Yabe District]], [[Settsu Province]], in [[Ōei]] 4 (1397).
== Festivals == The Okina Dance Ritual (翁舞神事), held annually on [[January 14]], is designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].
== Goshuin (Stamp) == * Goshuin can only be obtained by contacting the Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine representative's residence, as designated by the managing Gion Shrine. Gion Shrine itself does not issue the goshuin.
== Access == * A 17-minute walk north from [[Myōhōji Station]] on the [[Kobe Municipal Subway Seishin-Yamate Line]].
== Related Articles == * [[Narabehi Shrine]] (Okina Dance)
== External Links == * [http://www.kobe-gionjinjya.com/ohtoshi.html Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine] (via Gion Shrine)
{{citation needed|Kuruma Otoshi Shrine}}
{{shinto-stub}}

revision.comment: Bot: single‑shot reformat + ill→|12=draft + draft categories + iws + DEFAULTSORT + move HTML comments + meta‑cat check
17 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:車大歳神社 up to rev 75868058
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 車大歳神社 |画像 = [[File:Kuruma-ohtoshi-jinja.jpg|300px]] |所在地 = [[兵庫県]][[神戸市]][[須磨区]]車字松ヶ原551 {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|41|0.9|N|135|6|49.8|E|region:JP-28-type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[大歳御祖神]] |社格 = |創建 = [[大化]]2年（[[646年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[10月16日]] |神事 = 翁舞神事（[[1月14日]]） |地図={{Location map|Kobe#Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=41|lat_sec=0.9|lon_deg=135|lon_min=6|lon_sec=49.8|label=車大歳神社|position=center|width=200|float=center}}}}
'''車大歳神社'''（くるまおおとしじんじゃ）は、[[兵庫県]][[神戸市]][[須磨区]]車にある[[神社]]である。 現在は神職が常駐しておらず、[[兵庫区]]上祇園町の祇園神社宮司が兼務している。
== 祭神 == * [[年神|大歳御祖神]]（おおとしみおやのかみ）を祀る。 ==歴史== 由緒は不明であるが、『武庫郡誌』には[[大化]]2年（[[646年]]）の創建と記載されている。[[応永]]4年（[[1397年]]）、[[摂津国]][[八部郡]]丹生郷より遷座したとも伝えられる。
==祭事== 毎年[[1月14日]]に行われる[[車大歳神社の翁舞|翁舞神事]]は、[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定されている。
== ご朱印 == *ご朱印は、兼務社である祇園神社でやっておらず、祇園神社が指定した車大歳神社総代宅へ電話連絡することになる。
== 交通アクセス == *[[神戸市営地下鉄西神・山手線]][[妙法寺駅 (兵庫県)|妙法寺駅]]より、北に徒歩17分。
== 関連項目 == *[[奈良豆比古神社]]（[[奈良豆比古神社の翁舞|翁舞]]）
==外部リンク== *[http://www.kobe-gionjinjya.com/ohtoshi.html 車大歳神社]（祇園神社による）
{{DEFAULTSORT:くるまおおとししんしや}} [[Category:神戸市の神社]] [[Category:須磨区の歴史]] {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:須磨区の建築物]]

== 祭神 == * [[年神|大歳御祖神]]（おおとしみおやのかみ）を祀る。 ==歴史== 由緒は不明であるが、『武庫郡誌』には[[大化]]2年（[[646年]]）の創建と記載されている。[[応永]]4年（[[1397年]]）、[[摂津国]][[八部郡]]丹生郷より遷座したとも伝えられる。
==祭事== 毎年[[1月14日]]に行われる[[車大歳神社の翁舞|翁舞神事]]は、[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定されている。
== ご朱印 == *ご朱印は、兼務社である祇園神社でやっておらず、祇園神社が指定した車大歳神社総代宅へ電話連絡することになる。
== 交通アクセス == *[[神戸市営地下鉄西神・山手線]][[妙法寺駅 (兵庫県)|妙法寺駅]]より、北に徒歩17分。
== 関連項目 == *[[奈良豆比古神社]]（[[奈良豆比古神社の翁舞|翁舞]]）
==外部リンク== *[http://www.kobe-gionjinjya.com/ohtoshi.html 車大歳神社]（祇園神社による）
{{DEFAULTSORT:くるまおおとししんしや}} [[Category:神戸市の神社]] [[Category:須磨区の歴史]] {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:須磨区の建築物]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kuruma-ohtoshi-jinja.jpg|300px]] |location = [[Hyogo Prefecture]], [[Kobe City]], [[Suma Ward]], Kuruma Matsugahara 551 {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|41|0.9|N|135|6|49.8|E|region:JP-28-type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = [[Toshigami|Ōtoshi Mioya no Kami]] |Shrine Rank = |consecration_year = [[Taika]] 2 ([[646]]) |Main hall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = [[October 16]] |Rituals = Okina Dance Ritual ([[January 14]]) |Map = {{location map|Kobe#Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=41|lat_sec=0.9|lon_deg=135|lon_min=6|lon_sec=49.8|label=Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }}
'''Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine''' (車大歳神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the Kuruma district of [[Suma Ward]], [[Kobe City]], [[Hyogo Prefecture]]. Currently, it does not have a resident priest; the chief priest of [[Gion Shrine]] in Kamigion-chō, [[Hyogo Ward]], oversees its administration.
== deity == * [[Toshigami|Ōtoshi Mioya no Kami]] (大歳御祖神)
== History == While the origins of the shrine are unclear, ''Mukogun-shi'' records its founding in the second year of [[Taika]] (646). It is also said to have been relocated from Nyūgo, [[Yabe District]], [[Settsu Province]], in [[Ōei]] 4 (1397).
== Festivals == The Okina Dance Ritual (翁舞神事), held annually on [[January 14]], is designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].
== Goshuin (Stamp) == * Goshuin can only be obtained by contacting the Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine representative's residence, as designated by the managing Gion Shrine. Gion Shrine itself does not issue the goshuin.
== Access == * A 17-minute walk north from [[Myōhōji Station]] on the [[Kobe Municipal Subway Seishin-Yamate Line]].
== Related Articles == * [[Narabehi Shrine]] (Okina Dance)
== External Links == * [http://www.kobe-gionjinjya.com/ohtoshi.html Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine] (via Gion Shrine)

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kuruma-ohtoshi-jinja.jpg|300px]] |location = [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Kobe City]], [[Suma Ward]], Kuruma Matsugahara 551 {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|41|0.9|N|135|6|49.8|E|region:JP-28-type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = [[Toshigami|Ōtoshi Mioya no Kami]] |Shrine Rank = |consecration_year = [[Taika]] 2 ([[646]]) |Main hall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = [[October 16]] |Rituals = Okina Dance Ritual ([[January 14]]) |Map = {{location map|Kobe#Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=41|lat_sec=0.9|lon_deg=135|lon_min=6|lon_sec=49.8|label=Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }}
'''Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine''' (車大歳神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the Kuruma district of [[Suma Ward]], [[Kobe City]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]]. Currently, it does not have a resident priest; the chief priest of [[Gion Shrine]] in Kamigion-chō, [[Hyōgo Ward]], oversees its administration.
== deity == * [[Toshigami|Ōtoshi Mioya no Kami]] (大歳御祖神)
== History == While the origins of the shrine are unclear, ''Mukogun-shi'' records its founding in the second year of [[Taika]] (646). It is also said to have been relocated from Nyūgo, [[Yabe District]], [[Settsu Province]], in [[Ōei]] 4 (1397).
== Festivals == The Okina Dance Ritual (翁舞神事), held annually on [[January 14]], is designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].
== Goshuin (Stamp) == * Goshuin can only be obtained by contacting the Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine representative's residence, as designated by the managing Gion Shrine. Gion Shrine itself does not issue the goshuin.
== Access == * A 17-minute walk north from [[Myōhōji Station]] on the [[Kobe Municipal Subway Seishin-Yamate Line]].
== Related Articles == * [[Narabehi Shrine]] (Okina Dance)
== External Links == * [http://www.kobe-gionjinjya.com/ohtoshi.html Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine] (via Gion Shrine)
{{shinto-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|車大歳神社|version=75868058|comment=Imported full ja history}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kuruma-Ōtoshi-jinja]][[ja:車大歳神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11637295]] Wikidata name: Kuruma-Ōtoshi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kuruma-ohtoshi-jinja.jpg |location = [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[:en:Kobe City|Kobe City]], [[Suma Ward]], Kuruma Matsugahara 551 {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|41|0.9|N|135|6|49.8|E|region:JP-28-type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = [[Toshigami|Ōtoshi Mioya no Kami]] |Shrine Rank = |consecration_year = [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika (era)]] 2 ([[:en:646|646]]) |Main hall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = [[October 16]] |Rituals = Okina Dance Ritual ([[:en:January 14|January 14]]) |Map = {{location map|Kobe#Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=41|lat_sec=0.9|lon_deg=135|lon_min=6|lon_sec=49.8|label=Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} |location = {{ill|Suma-ku|WD=Q1155010}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.683583|N|135.113833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Toshigami|WD=Q3028663}} }}
'''Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine''' (車大歳神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the Kuruma district of [[Suma Ward]], [[:en:Kobe City|Kobe City]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. Currently, it does not have a resident priest; the chief priest of [[Gion Shrine]] in Kamigion-chō, [[Hyōgo Ward]], oversees its administration.
== deity == * [[Toshigami|Ōtoshi Mioya no Kami]] (大歳御祖神)
== History == While the origins of the shrine are unclear, ''Mukogun-shi'' records its founding in the second year of [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika (era)]] (646). It is also said to have been relocated from Nyūgo, [[Yabe District]], [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]], in [[:en:Ōei|Ōei]] 4 (1397).
== Festivals == The Okina Dance Ritual (翁舞神事), held annually on [[:en:January 14|January 14]], is designated as an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].
== Goshuin (Stamp) == * Goshuin can only be obtained by contacting the Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine representative's residence, as designated by the managing Gion Shrine. Gion Shrine itself does not issue the goshuin.
== Access == * A 17-minute walk north from [[:en:Myōhōji Station|Myōhōji Station]] on the [[Kobe Municipal Subway Seishin-Yamate Line]].
== Related Articles == * [[Narabehi Shrine]] (Okina Dance)
== External Links == * [http://www.kobe-gionjinjya.com/ohtoshi.html Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine] (via Gion Shrine)
{{shinto-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|車大歳神社|version=75868058|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kobe]]
[[Category:Completion tier 2]]
}}
[[ja:車大歳神社]]
[[ja:久留真神社]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kuruma-Ōtoshi-jinja]][[ja:車大歳神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11637295]] Wikidata name: Kuruma-Ōtoshi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kuruma-ohtoshi-jinja.jpg |location = [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[:en:Kobe City|Kobe City]], [[Suma Ward]], Kuruma Matsugahara 551 {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|41|0.9|N|135|6|49.8|E|region:JP-28-type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = [[Toshigami|Ōtoshi Mioya no Kami]] |Shrine Rank = |consecration_year = [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika (era)]] 2 ([[:en:646|646]]) |Main hall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = [[October 16]] |Rituals = Okina Dance Ritual ([[:en:January 14|January 14]]) |Map = {{location map|Kobe#Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=41|lat_sec=0.9|lon_deg=135|lon_min=6|lon_sec=49.8|label=Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} |location = {{ill|Suma-ku|WD=Q1155010}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.683583|N|135.113833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Toshigami|WD=Q3028663}} }}
'''Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine''' (車大歳神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the Kuruma district of [[Suma Ward]], [[:en:Kobe City|Kobe City]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. Currently, it does not have a resident priest; the chief priest of [[Gion Shrine]] in Kamigion-chō, [[Hyōgo Ward]], oversees its administration.
== deity == * [[Toshigami|Ōtoshi Mioya no Kami]] (大歳御祖神)
== History == While the origins of the shrine are unclear, ''Mukogun-shi'' records its founding in the second year of [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika (era)]] (646). It is also said to have been relocated from Nyūgo, [[Yabe District]], [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]], in [[:en:Ōei|Ōei]] 4 (1397).
== Festivals == The Okina Dance Ritual (翁舞神事), held annually on [[:en:January 14|January 14]], is designated as an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].
== Goshuin (Stamp) == * Goshuin can only be obtained by contacting the Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine representative's residence, as designated by the managing Gion Shrine. Gion Shrine itself does not issue the goshuin.
== Access == * A 17-minute walk north from [[:en:Myōhōji Station|Myōhōji Station]] on the [[Kobe Municipal Subway Seishin-Yamate Line]].
== Related Articles == * [[Narabehi Shrine]] (Okina Dance)
== External Links == * [http://www.kobe-gionjinjya.com/ohtoshi.html Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine] (via Gion Shrine)
{{shinto-stub}}

Currently, it does not have a resident priest; the chief priest of [[Gion Shrine]] in Kamigion-chō, [[Hyōgo Ward]], oversees its administration.
== deity == * [[Toshigami|Ōtoshi Mioya no Kami]] (大歳御祖神)
== History == While the origins of the shrine are unclear, ''Mukogun-shi'' records its founding in the second year of [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika (era)]] (646). It is also said to have been relocated from Nyūgo, [[Yabe District]], [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]], in [[:en:Ōei|Ōei]] 4 (1397).
== Festivals == The Okina Dance Ritual (翁舞神事), held annually on [[:en:January 14|January 14]], is designated as an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].
== Goshuin (Stamp) == * Goshuin can only be obtained by contacting the Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine representative's residence, as designated by the managing Gion Shrine. Gion Shrine itself does not issue the goshuin.

== Related Articles == * [[Narabehi Shrine]] (Okina Dance)
== External Links == * [http://www.kobe-gionjinjya.com/ohtoshi.html Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine] (via Gion Shrine)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kuruma-Ōtoshi-jinja]][[ja:車大歳神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kuruma-Ōtoshi-jinja]][[ja:車大歳神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kuruma-Ōtoshi-jinja]][[ja:車大歳神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11637295]] Wikidata name: Kuruma-Ōtoshi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kuruma-ohtoshi-jinja.jpg |location = [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[:en:Kobe City|Kobe City]], [[Suma Ward]], Kuruma Matsugahara 551 {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|41|0.9|N|135|6|49.8|E|region:JP-28-type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = [[Toshigami|Ōtoshi Mioya no Kami]] |Shrine Rank = |consecration_year = [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika (era)]] 2 ([[:en:646|646]]) |Main hall = |Alias = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = [[October 16]] |Rituals = Okina Dance Ritual ([[:en:January 14|January 14]]) |Map = {{location map|Kobe#Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=41|lat_sec=0.9|lon_deg=135|lon_min=6|lon_sec=49.8|label=Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} |location = {{ill|Suma-ku|WD=Q1155010}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.683583|N|135.113833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Toshigami|WD=Q3028663}} }}
'''Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine''' (車大歳神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the Kuruma district of [[Suma Ward]], [[:en:Kobe City|Kobe City]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. Currently, it does not have a resident priest; the chief priest of [[Gion Shrine]] in Kamigion-chō, [[Hyōgo Ward]], oversees its administration.
== deity == * [[Toshigami|Ōtoshi Mioya no Kami]] (大歳御祖神)
== History == While the origins of the shrine are unclear, ''Mukogun-shi'' records its founding in the second year of [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika (era)]] (646). It is also said to have been relocated from Nyūgo, [[Yabe District]], [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]], in [[:en:Ōei|Ōei]] 4 (1397).
== Festivals == The Okina Dance Ritual (翁舞神事), held annually on [[:en:January 14|January 14]], is designated as an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].
== Goshuin (Stamp) == * Goshuin can only be obtained by contacting the Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine representative's residence, as designated by the managing Gion Shrine. Gion Shrine itself does not issue the goshuin.
== Access == * A 17-minute walk north from [[:en:Myōhōji Station|Myōhōji Station]] on the [[Kobe Municipal Subway Seishin-Yamate Line]].
== Related Articles == * [[Narabehi Shrine]] (Okina Dance)
== External Links == * [http://www.kobe-gionjinjya.com/ohtoshi.html Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine] (via Gion Shrine)
{{shinto-stub}}

[[ja:車大歳神社]]
[[ja:久留真神社]]

Currently, it does not have a resident priest; the chief priest of [[Gion Shrine]] in Kamigion-chō, [[Hyōgo Ward]], oversees its administration.
== deity == * [[Toshigami|Ōtoshi Mioya no Kami]] (大歳御祖神)
== History == While the origins of the shrine are unclear, ''Mukogun-shi'' records its founding in the second year of {{ill|Taika (era)|en|Taika (era)|WD=Q1077930|lt=Taika (era)}} (646). It is also said to have been relocated from Nyūgo, [[Yabe District]], {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|WD=Q864163|lt=Settsu Province}}, in {{ill|Ōei|en|Ōei|WD=Q1193078|lt=Ōei}} 4 (1397).
== Festivals == The Okina Dance Ritual (翁舞神事), held annually on {{ill|January 14|en|January 14|WD=Q2257|lt=January 14}}, is designated as an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].
== Goshuin (Stamp) == * Goshuin can only be obtained by contacting the Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine representative's residence, as designated by the managing Gion Shrine. Gion Shrine itself does not issue the goshuin.
== Access == * A 17-minute walk north from {{ill|Myōhōji Station|en|Myōhōji Station|WD=Q6949382|lt=Myōhōji Station}} on the [[Kobe Municipal Subway Seishin-Yamate Line]].
== Related Articles == * [[Narabehi Shrine]] (Okina Dance)
== External Links == * [http://www.kobe-gionjinjya.com/ohtoshi.html Kuruma Ōtoshi Shrine] (via Gion Shrine)
